zh.Litchfield, Colony of Connecticut, Briti: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州利其費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Way於1738年9月16日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州利其費爾德時，他的父親David Waye38歲，母親Esther Russell39歲。他於1761年9月1日與Sarah Lewis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文沃特波利結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文梅利登住了大約30年。他去世於1806年12月在美國康乃狄克州利其費爾德普萊姆斯普萊姆斯 霍洛福享年68歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州利其費爾德普萊姆斯托馬斯頓希爾希德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Samuel Way于1738年9月16日出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州利其費爾德时，他的父亲David Waye38岁，母亲Esther Russell39岁。他于1761年9月1日与Sarah Lewis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文沃特波利结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文梅利登住了大约30年。他去世于1806年12月在美国康涅狄格州利其費爾德普萊姆斯普萊姆斯 霍洛福享年68岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州利其費爾德普萊姆斯托馬斯頓希爾希德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Way
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Wey
meanings: English (Dorset and Somerset): variant of   Way  .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Weij, a topographic name from weide ‘pasture, meadow’ (compare Vanderweide ).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names based on the element wīg ‘battle’ (compare Weyers ). This surname is also found in Belgium.

variants.name: Waye
meanings: English (Devon and Kent): variant of   Way  .

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

historicEvents.description: Created on October 9, 1751 from Fairfield, New Haven and   Hartford Counties
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: British Forces Capture Fort Griswold
historicEvents.description: The capture of Fort Griswold was the final act of treason that Benedict Arnold committed. This would be a British victory. On the American side 85 were killed, 35 wounded and paroled, 28 taken prisoner, 13 escaped, and 1 twelve year old was captured and released.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History of the Battle of Groton and The Burning of New London

historicEvents.description: Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.

"Watertown, Northbury Parish in 1793...""The history of Waterbury, Connecticut; the original township embracing present Watertown and Plymouth, and parts of Oxford, Wolcott, Middlebury, Prospect and Naugatuck. With an appendix of biography, genealogy and statistics"" by Bronson, Henry, 1804"

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Way was born on 16 September 1738, in Litchfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, his father, David Waye, was 38 and his mother, Esther Russell, was 39. He married Sarah Lewis on 1 September 1761, in Waterbury, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 6 daughters. He lived in Meriden, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America for about 30 years. He died in December 1806, in Plymouth Hollow, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, United States, at the age of 68, and was buried in Hillside Cemetery, Thomaston, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Samuel Way est né le 16 septembre 1738, à Litchfield, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, David Waye, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Esther Russell, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Lewis le 1 septembre 1761, à Waterbury, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 6 filles. Il a vécu à Meriden, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique pendant environ 30 ans. Il est décédé en décembre 1806, à Plymouth Hollow, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, États-Unis, à l’âge de 68 ans, et a été enterré à Hillside Cemetery, Thomaston, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Samuel Way am 16. September 1738 in Litchfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater David Waye 38 und seine Mutter Esther Russell 39. Er hat am 1. September 1761 in Waterbury, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Sarah Lewis geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er hat ungefähr 30 Jahre lang in Meriden, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. it.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Way è nato il il 16 settembre 1738 nel Litchfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, David Waye, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Esther Russell, aveva 39 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Lewis il 1 settembre 1761, nel Waterbury, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 6 figlie. Vissuto a Meriden, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America per circa 30 anni. Morì nel dicembre 1806, nel Plymouth Hollow, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 68 anni, e fu sepolto a Hillside Cemetery, Thomaston, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Samuel Wayが1738年9月16日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州リトチフィールドで生まれた時，父David Wayeは38歳，母Esther Russellは39歳であった。1761年9月1日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンワテルブリーでSarah Lewisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンメリデンに約30年間住んでいた。彼は1806年12月にアメリカ合衆国コネチカット州リトチフィールドプリモウスプリモウス ホーロウで68歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州リトチフィールドプリモウスソマストンヒルシド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Thomaston, Litchfield, Connecticut, Unit: 미국 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드 플림우스 플림우스 홀로

ko.Litchfield, Colony of Connecticut, Briti: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드
ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Way(이)가 1738년 9월 16일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 David Waye(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Esther Russell(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1761년 9월 1일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 왓르버이에서 Sarah Lewis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 머이든에서 약 30년 동안 살았다. 그는 1806년 12월 미국 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드 플림우스 플림우스 홀로에서 68세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 리트ch필드 플림우스 톰아스턴 힐시드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Way родился 16 сентября 1738 г. в местности личфиелд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени David Waye было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Esther Russell, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Lewis 1 сентября 1761 г. в местности вэитирбури, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он жил в местности мириден, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика около 30 лет. Он умер декабрь 1806 г. в местности плимоут холлоу, плимоут, личфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 68 лет и был похоронен в местности хиллсид кеметири, томастон, плимоут, личфиелд, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Samuel Way nació el 16 de septiembre de 1738, en Litchfield, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, David Waye, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Esther Russell, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Sarah Lewis el 1 de septiembre de 1761, en Waterbury, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 6 hijas. Vivió en Meriden, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America durante unos 30 años. Murió en diciembre de 1806, en Plymouth Hollow, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 68 años, y fue sepultado en Hillside Cemetery, Thomaston, Plymouth, Litchfield, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
